claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 3 years old and does not live with you.
  - Blake is 2 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 3 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 2 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Someone who lives with the child is already claiming
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 3 (under 16); Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's start the eligibility assessment.

**First, I need to know about the child (or children) you're claiming for.**

**How old is the child?**

- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

If you have multiple children, let's start with one and then we can check the others. Please tell me the child's name and age.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi! Yes, I'm looking to find out if I can claim Child Benefit. I've got three kids actually - Alex who's 3, Blake who's 2, and Charlie who's also 3.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you! So you have three children:
- **Alex** - 3 years old
- **Blake** - 2 years old
- **Charlie** - 3 years old

Since all three children are under 16, they automatically meet the age requirement for Child Benefit. Let me check the next eligibility criteria for them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
This question is about whether the children are working 24 or more hours per week. Given that Alex, Blake, and Charlie are all aged 2-3, they obviously won't be in paid employment!

So the answer is **yes** - they are all working less than 24 hours per week (i.e., not working at all). Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
This asks whether the children are receiving certain benefits (like Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit) in their own right. At ages 2 and 3, your children wouldn't be receiving these benefits.

So the answer is **yes** - they are NOT receiving these benefits. Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: This checks about apprenticeships. Since your children are all aged 2-3, they are clearly not in any apprenticeship.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check your residency status.

**Do you meet any of the following UK residency requirements?**

You qualify if **any one** of these applies to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK**
2. **You have settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. **You have pre-settled status** and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold for 3+ continuous months)
4. **You have pre-settled status** and are actively seeking work (can claim for up to 91 days)
5. **You have pre-settled status** with sufficient resources to support yourself (and not claiming certain means-tested benefits)
6. **You have pre-settled status** and are studying with sufficient resources
7. **You have pre-settled status** as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national with right to reside
8. **You're a Crown servant** living abroad (or in certain qualifying countries)

Do any of these apply to you? For most people, the answer is simply "I normally live in the UK."

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, you normally live in the UK (Manchester), so you meet the residency requirement. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check your responsibility for the children.

**Are you responsible for Alex, Blake, and Charlie?**

You qualify if **any one** of these applies:

1. **You live with the children** - They live in your home with you
2. **You contribute to their upkeep** - You pay at least the equivalent of Child Benefit towards their care (food, clothes, pocket money, housing, etc.)
3. **You pay maintenance** - Under a court order or agreement that covers their upkeep

Do you live with all three children, or are you responsible for them in another way?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Blake lives with me at home. But Alex and Charlie don't live with me - they live with their other parent.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. That's an important distinction. Let me assess each child's situation:

- **Blake (age 2)** - Lives with you ✓
- **Alex (age 3)** - Lives with their other parent
- **Charlie (age 3)** - Lives with their other parent

For **Blake**, since they live with you, you clearly meet the responsibility requirement.

For **Alex and Charlie**, since they don't live with you, I need to ask:

**Do you contribute to Alex and Charlie's upkeep?** 

This could include:
- Paying maintenance (under a court order or agreement)
- Contributing at least the equivalent of Child Benefit towards their care (food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents, etc.)
- This can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

Do you make any such contributions for Alex and/or Charlie?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I do help out with Alex - I contribute towards their upkeep, buying clothes and helping with other expenses. But for Charlie, no, I don't contribute anything - their mum handles everything.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for being clear about that. So the situation is:

- **Blake (age 2)** - Lives with you ✓ (You're responsible)
- **Alex (age 3)** - You contribute to their upkeep ✓ (You're responsible)
- **Charlie (age 3)** - You don't live with them and don't contribute ✗ (Not responsible)

Unfortunately, for **Charlie**, since you neither live with them nor contribute to their upkeep, you wouldn't be eligible to claim Child Benefit for Charlie. Their other parent (who they live with) would be the one who could claim.

Let me continue the assessment for **Blake** and **Alex**, for whom you are responsible.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check about other claimants.

**Is anyone else currently claiming (or planning to claim) Child Benefit for Blake or Alex?**

For example:
- Is Blake's other parent claiming Child Benefit for Blake?
- Is Alex's other parent (who Alex lives with) claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

This is important because only one person can receive Child Benefit for each child. If there's a dispute about who should claim, HMRC will need to decide.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
For Blake, no one else is claiming - it's just me who would be claiming for Blake.

But for Alex, yes, their other parent is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. This is important information:

- **Blake (age 2)** - No other claimant ✓ We can continue the assessment
- **Alex (age 3)** - Their other parent is already claiming Child Benefit

For **Alex**, since their other parent is already claiming Child Benefit and Alex lives with them, the situation is:

**Would you and Alex's other parent agree to transfer the Child Benefit claim to you, or would you want to dispute who should receive it?**

Generally, when a child lives with one parent and another parent contributes to their upkeep, the person the child **lives with** usually has priority for the claim. However, if both parties agree, the claim can be transferred.

What's the situation - would you:
1. **Accept that the other parent continues to claim** for Alex (since Alex lives with them)
2. **Both agree to transfer** the claim to you
3. **Want to dispute** who should receive the benefit (HMRC would then decide)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh, I see. Well, Alex lives with their mum and she's already claiming, so I suppose she should continue claiming for Alex. I'm not looking to dispute it or anything.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That makes perfect sense. Since Alex lives with their mum and she's already claiming Child Benefit for Alex, it's appropriate for her to continue receiving it.

So to summarise where we are:

- **Blake (age 2)** - Continuing assessment ✓
- **Alex (age 3)** - Their other parent is already claiming (and you're happy for this to continue) - **You won't claim for Alex**
- **Charlie (age 3)** - You're not responsible for them - **You won't claim for Charlie**

Let me continue the assessment for **Blake**.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Where is Blake currently living?**

Is Blake:
1. **Living at home with you normally** (standard living arrangement)
2. **In hospital**
3. **In local authority care**
4. **Being fostered**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Blake's living at home with me - just a normal living arrangement.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Almost there! Just one final question for Blake:

**Is the local council NOT paying towards Blake's accommodation or maintenance?**

In other words, is Blake's care fully funded by you (not by the local council)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - I'm the one paying for everything for Blake. The council isn't paying anything towards their care.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! That's all the questions I need to ask. Let me now provide you with the final eligibility outcome.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
---

## 🎉 Child Benefit Eligibility Assessment Complete

Here's your summary:

| Child | Age | Eligible for You to Claim? | Reason |
|-------|-----|---------------------------|--------|
| **Blake** | 2 | ✅ **YES** | You live with Blake, are responsible for their care, and meet all eligibility requirements |
| **Alex** | 3 | ❌ No | Their other parent is already claiming and will continue to do so |
| **Charlie** | 3 | ❌ No | You don't live with them or contribute to their upkeep |

---

### Next Steps for Claiming Child Benefit for Blake:

1. **Apply online or by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and Blake
3. **Provide Blake's birth certificate** (or adoption certificate if applicable)
4. **Be aware of the High Income Child Benefit Charge** - if you or your partner earns over £60,000, you may have to pay some or all of the benefit back through tax
5. **Payments** are made every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)

📎 **Apply here:** [https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim)

---

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit or the application process?

reasonings: Blake (age 2) meets all eligibility criteria: (1) Under 16 years old - automatically qualifies on age; (2) Not working 24+ hours per week; (3) Not receiving benefits in their own right; (4) Not in an apprenticeship; (5) You meet UK residency requirements (living in Manchester); (6) You are responsible for Blake as they live with you; (7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake; (8) Blake lives at home in a normal living arrangement; (9) The local council is not paying towards Blake's care. All criteria satisfied - ELIGIBLE.

Alex (age 3) - Although Alex meets the age requirement and you contribute to their upkeep (making you technically responsible), their other parent is already claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Since Alex lives with their other parent and you have agreed that she should continue claiming, you cannot also claim for Alex. Only one person can receive Child Benefit per child - NOT ELIGIBLE for you to claim.

Charlie (age 3) - Although Charlie meets the age requirement, you are not responsible for Charlie. You do not live with Charlie and you do not contribute to their upkeep. Without meeting the responsibility requirement, you cannot claim Child Benefit for Charlie - NOT ELIGIBLE for you to claim.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake only. For Alex, their other parent is already claiming and will continue to do so. For Charlie, you do not meet the responsibility requirement as you neither live with them nor contribute to their upkeep - Charlie's other parent would be the appropriate claimant for Charlie.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?
response.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
response.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

response.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location
response.routing_logic: Check if child is in hospital, care, fostered, or living normally

response.question: Is the local council NOT paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance?
response.help_text: You generally cannot get Child Benefit if the local council is paying for the child

response.description: Applicant meets all criteria for Child Benefit
response.reason: Meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements
next_steps: Apply online or by post using form CH2
Provide proof of identity for yourself and the child
Provide child's birth certificate or adoption certificate
Be aware of High Income Child Benefit Charge if you or partner earns over £60,000
Payments made every 4 weeks (or weekly if single parent or receiving certain benefits)

child_evaluations.reasoning: Blake (age 2) meets all eligibility criteria: (1) Under 16 years old - automatically qualifies on age; (2) Not working 24+ hours per week; (3) Not receiving benefits in their own right; (4) Not in an apprenticeship; (5) You meet UK residency requirements (living in Manchester); (6) You are responsible for Blake as they live with you; (7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake; (8) Blake lives at home in a normal living arrangement; (9) The local council is not paying towards Blake's care. All criteria satisfied - ELIGIBLE.

Alex (age 3) - Although Alex meets the age requirement and you contribute to their upkeep (making you technically responsible), their other parent is already claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Since Alex lives with their other parent and you have agreed that she should continue claiming, you cannot also claim for Alex. Only one person can receive Child Benefit per child - NOT ELIGIBLE for you to claim.

Charlie (age 3) - Although Charlie meets the age requirement, you are not responsible for Charlie. You do not live with Charlie and you do not contribute to their upkeep. Without meeting the responsibility requirement, you cannot claim Child Benefit for Charlie - NOT ELIGIBLE for you to claim.

response.overall_reasoning: You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake only. For Alex, their other parent is already claiming and will continue to do so. For Charlie, you do not meet the responsibility requirement as you neither live with them nor contribute to their upkeep - Charlie's other parent would be the appropriate claimant for Charlie.
